a:
Patient presents with:
Cough: Accompanied by
X 2 day.BF
Runny nose: X 1 day.BF
Vomiting: Strated yesterday.B
Ear pain: Per dad pt already has 2 ear infection in the past and was given antibiotic per dad pt is still
pulling ears, BF
Immunization: Mmmr,varicella and Hep A, if pt is doing well and if okay with the provider.E BF
HPI: Patient has been coughing for the past 2 days along with runny nose. He vomited once last night
after finishing a bottle of milk before going to sleep. No blood in vomit. No diarrhea. Denies fever. Patient
consistently tugs on his ears and has history of recurrent ear infections.
Patient has had 4 episodes of recurrent bilateral/right otitis media:
7/15/2025: tx with Augmentin
7/26/2025: tx with Augmentin (Urgent care- thought that previous tx was with Amoxicillin, but it was
Augmentin)
10/08/2025 : tx with Amoxcillin (right AOM)
11/12/2025 (today): tx with Cefdinir
PMH:

CHEST/LUNGS: symmetric expansion, no subcostal retractions or tracheal tugging, clear to auscultation

ABDOMEN: normoactive bowel sounds, soft, non-tender, non-distended, no mass or organomegaly

claude_vision.referral_reason: Recurrent acute suppurative otitis media (bilateral) without spontaneous rupture of tympanic membrane — referred to ENT for evaluation and consideration of PE (pressure equalization) tubes due to 4 episodes of recurrent ear infections within approximately 4 months.
relevant_clinical_findings: 4 episodes of recurrent bilateral/right otitis media between 7/15/2025 and 11/12/2025
Episode 2 — 7/26/2025: treated with Augmentin (urgent care visit)
Episode 4 — 11/12/2025 (day of referral): treated with Cefdinir
Physical exam on 11/12/2025: Bilateral TM with erythema and purulent effusion, nasal congestion, copious clear rhinorrhea, oropharynx with mild erythema
Vitals on 11/12/2025: Pulse 121, Resp 33, Temp 98.6°F, SpO2 98%, Weight 10.8 kg (23 lb 13 oz) — tachycardia and tachypnea noted but afebrile
No significant past medical history on file
Referred by NP Garello, Morgan Drew at South Area Pediatric Medical Group Inc
ENT visit completed 2/10/2026 with Keaton, Evelyn Legaspi, NP — New Patient/Consultation (status: Completed)
Hearing Evaluation/Test also completed 2/10/2026 with Nunez, Catherine Noel, AUD (status: Completed)
missing_information: No audiology/hearing test results included in the faxed documents (hearing evaluation was scheduled and completed 2/10/2026, but results/report not attached)
No ENT consultation note from the 2/10/2026 completed visit attached
No prior antibiotic treatment records or urgent care notes attached to verify the episode history
Patient name and date of birth are redacted/illegible throughout the document, making patient identification dependent on system matching
Insurance authorization tracking number is partially obscured/illegible
Subscriber Name and Subscriber ID fields are blank on the eligibility verification page

provider_urgency_label.label: urgent
provider_urgency_label.source: Multiple locations: (1) Referral Information section — Priority field states 'Urgent'; (2) Scheduling Comments note dated 12/3/2025 references an attachment labeled 'URG'; (3) Internal message visible in scheduling comments dated 12/2/2025 references 'RE: Urgent Referral — EXPO'

criteria_check.provider_label: Urgent — marked in Priority field of referral and referenced as 'Urgent Referral' in internal messaging
criteria_check.reasoning: Although the referring provider labeled this referral as urgent and it was internally messaged as an urgent referral, none of the defined ENT urgent clinical criteria are met — there is no sudden hearing loss, airway compromise, malignancy, abscess, nasal fracture, foreign body, or infant feeding/tongue tie issue — making this a SECONDARY APPROVAL NEEDED case where the triage team should confirm whether the provider's urgency label is clinically justified or reflects practice-level concern about recurrence frequency.

claude_vision.summary: This is a pediatric referral to ENT for a young child (approximately toddler age based on weight of ~10.8 kg) with 4 documented episodes of recurrent acute bilateral otitis media between July and November 2025, treated with multiple antibiotic courses, and now being evaluated for possible PE tube placement. The referring NP labeled the referral as urgent, and the ENT visit along with a hearing evaluation were both completed on 2/10/2026 at Sacramento Ear, Nose, and Throat. No imaging was ordered, and while visits are marked completed, neither the ENT consultation note nor the hearing evaluation results are included in the faxed documents.
